  • 2002: Brazil Pan American games, silver
  • 2000- 1st Team All- American (Wisconsin- Whitewater)

Quick Facts
At the age of five, Pennie was injured due to three gunshot wounds to the side of his body in Houston, Texas. Due to the lack of any family members to care for him, he became a foster child and a resident at Hughen Center in Port Arthur, Texas. At 16 years old, Pennie was first introduced to wheelchair basketball by Randy Snow, the first Paralympian to be inducted into the U.S. Olympic Hall of Fame. Pennie is head coach of the Dallas Jr. Wheelchair Mavericks. He also has served as head coach of the Army team at the 2014 Warrior Games. He has also played professional wheelchair basketball in Spain and France.
Education:

Quick Facts
  • Born with a condition known as tibial hemimelia, resulting in the amputation of his right leg when he was four years old
  • Began swimming at age 9 and was named Virginia's Disability Swimmer of the Year in 2014
  • Swims at the University of Mary Washington
  • Son of Sandy and Jody Peppersack
  • Hobbies include playing the electric guitar, video games, and listening to music.
Career Highlights
  • 2019 World Para Swimming World Series Indianapolis, 2nd (100-meter freestyle, 100-meter backstroke), 3rd (400-meter freestyle), 4th (100-meter breaststroke)
  • 2018 World Para Swimming World Series Indianapolis, 2nd (100-meter backstroke, 50-meter freestyle), 3rd (100-meter freestyle)
  • 2017 Can-Am Open, 1st (50-meter freestyle, 100-meter backstroke, 100-meter freestyle)
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 7th (100m Backstroke S8 - Men), 8th (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Men), 9th (4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Pergolini began swimming in 2008 at age four and started competing competitively at age five
  • Growing up, she played lacrosse, soccer and gymnastics, but swimming was her passion
  • When she was diagnosed with Stargardt’s disease, she decided to focus on swimming
  • In 2017, Pergolini set multiple records, including the American and Pan Am record in the S13 100-meter backstroke at the 2017 U.S. Paralympics Swimming World Championships Trials in Colorado Springs, Colorado
  • Daughter of Bill and Alice Pergolini
  • Has two brothers, Matthew and David
  • Hobbies include hanging out with her friends, baking and listening to Ariana Grande.

    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (100m Backstroke S13 - Women), 5th (100m Butterfly S13 - Women), 9th (50m Freestyle S13 - Women)

bio.education: The Bishop’s School (LaJolla, Calif.) ‘08 Stanford University ’18, Earth Systems
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Perkins began swimming at age 12 when his family moved to California. His parents encouraged him to try many sports growing up, even though he was born without hands or feet. Their only rule was that he had to try a sport at least once before he said he could not do it. His family gave him swimming lessons as a safety precaution. He was then spotted by coach Don Watkinds when he competed at the San Diego Triathlon Challenge. Perkins has set the standard in the S5 classification, having been named the Best Male Swimmer of the Meet at the 2014 and 2015 Can-Am Championships. In 2008 he was the co-recipient (with Rudy Garcia-Tolson) of the Challenged Athlete of the Year award by the Challenged Athletes Foundation. He was named the 2006 Challenged Athlete Star of the Year by San Diego Hall of Champions
  • Son of Roy and Jana Perkins
  • Roy is an only child but has five dog “siblings”
  • His hobbies include playing basketball, following the Washington Redskins and Capitals, stand-up comedy, and food
  • Cousin Levi Knutson (University of Colorado) played professional basketball in Spain.
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian; 10-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 4 silver, 4 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (50m Butterfly S5 - Men), silver (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 200m Freestyle S5 - Men), bronze (50m Freestyle S5 - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, silver (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Butterfly S5 - Men), bronze (200m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Freestyle S5 - Men), 14th (50m Backstroke S5 - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2008, (4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Men), gold (50m Butterfly S5 - Men), bronze (100m Freestyle S5 - Men), 4th (200m Freestyle S5 - Men, 4 x 50m Medley Relay 20 Points - Men, 50m Freestyle S5 - Men), 5th (200m Individual Medley SM5 - Men), 10th (100m Breaststroke SB4 - Men), 13th (50m Backstroke S5 - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2015 – (100m Breaststroke SB4 - Men), silver (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Butterfly S5 - Men), bronze (200m Freestyle S5 - Men), 5th (50m Freestyle S5 - Men), 6th (4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Mixed)
  • Years of participation: 100m Breaststroke SB4 - Men 2015; 100m Freestyle S5 - Men 2006, 2010, 2013, 2015; 200m Freestyle S5 - Men 2006, 2010, 2013, 2015; 4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Mixed 2015; 50m Butterfly S5 - Men 2006, 2010, 2013, 2015; 50m Freestyle S5 - Men 2006, 2010, 2013, 2015; 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Men 2013; 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Men 2013; 4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Men 2006, 2010; 4 x 50m Medley Relay 20 Points - Men 2006, 2010; 50m Backstroke S5 - Men 2006, 2010; 200m Individual Medley SM5 - Men 2006
  • Medals: 14 (2 gold, 8 silver, 4 bronze)
    • Gold – 2013 (50m Butterfly S5 - Men); 2006 (50m Butterfly S5 - Men)
    • Silver – 2015 (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Butterfly S5 - Men); 2013 (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 200m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Freestyle S5 - Men); 2010 (100m Freestyle S5 - Men, 200m Freestyle S5 - Men, 50m Butterfly S5 - Men)
    • Bronze – 2015 (200m Freestyle S5 - Men); 2010 (50m Freestyle S5 - Men); 2006 (200m Individual Medley SM5 - Men, 4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Men)

Career Highlights

 

  • 2014 National Championships, 1st (skeet)
  • 2014 Tucson World Cup, bronze (skeet)
  • 2013 National Championships, 1st (skeet)
  • 2012 Junior National Championships, 1st (skeet)
  • 2011 National Junior Olympic Championships, 2nd (skeet)
